summary: Safety limitations are explicit and non-blocking for this lane: the proposed coverage assessment preserves thin cross-human safety coverage, metadata-only abstract boundaries for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk, full article/table extraction debt, D+Q supplement incompleteness, and a block on cross-human safety synthesis until adverse-event definitions and denominators are harmonized.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed coverage assessment marks safety_durability coverage as thin and states that cross-human safety remains thin despite improved D+Q bone registry and publication-table extraction.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents the coverage repair from being read as a mature or harmonized human safety synthesis.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the safety_durability thin label and associated gap note through promotion unless later full-text/table extraction closes the stated limitations.

findings.claim_or_issue: DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety details are bounded to metadata-only PubMed EFetch abstract snapshots, not retained full-text or table sources.
findings.why_it_matters: Abstract-only safety statements cannot support detailed adverse-event term, seriousness, relatedness, zero-semantics, or denominator harmonization.
findings.recommended_action: Preserve the full article/table extraction debt for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q supplement material before upgrading the safety evidence tier.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed assessment explicitly blocks cross-human safety synthesis until event definitions, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, and seriousness fields are harmonized.
findings.why_it_matters: The boundary avoids pooling heterogeneous pilot, registry, abstract, and table-derived safety data into an unsupported quantitative or comparative safety claim.
findings.recommended_action: Do not create a cross-human safety compatibility group until the named harmonization conditions are source-backed and reviewed.
